results.query: i wrote a python script that analyzes my aws bill by service and prints a table, but it looks like garbage — plain text columns that don't line up. can you make the output actually readable, aligned columns, and color the expensive services red?
building a little cli that pulls my github notifications and summarizes them. right now it just dumps json to stdout. i want it to print a nice summary in the terminal when it finishes
i have a benchmark runner and i want a progress section that shows a [PASS]/[FAIL] tag as each test runs, then a summary table at the end with the timings

saw someone post a terminal screenshot where each row had a little colored bar next to it showing relative magnitude, like a mini chart inside the table. how do i make my analysis script print output like that?
my data pipeline prints a wall of log lines and i can never tell where it is. i'd like section headers and to dim the less important stuff so the key numbers stand out
write a cli tool that reads a folder of csv files and reports row counts and null percentages per column — make the output look polished, not just bare print statements
i'm scoring 200 model checkpoints and want to show a leaderboard in the terminal sorted by accuracy, with a bar next to each one so i can eyeball the spread
can you add a little sparkline showing the 7-day trend next to each metric in my `status` command output?
the output of my deploy script is one giant blob. break it into sections with headers and use green/red so i can see at a glance what succeeded and what failed
make a terminal dashboard that summarizes my running cloud costs by region, with the biggest spenders highlighted
build me a web dashboard in react that charts our daily active users with recharts and a date-range picker
i have a matplotlib script already — export the revenue-over-time chart as a high-res png so i can drop it into the board slide deck
my iterm2 colors look washed out and the comments are unreadable. help me pick a better color scheme / theme for my terminal
strip all the ansi escape codes out of this captured CI log file so i can paste clean text into a jira ticket
set up structured json logging with the python logging module and ship the logs to datadog with correlation ids
review this chart i made and tell me whether it follows good data-viz principles — is the truncated y-axis misleading the reader?
build an interactive TUI with textual that has a sidebar, a scrollable log pane, and keyboard navigation between widgets
convert this messy sales.xlsx into a clean csv and add a profit_margin column computed from revenue and cost
generate a pdf invoice from this order json with our company logo at the top and a totals table
explain how 256-color ansi escape codes work and how they differ from 24-bit truecolor
